vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1224: surface a failed post-merge dashboard deploy loudly (stale-code signal)

plan.title: Surface a failed post-merge dashboard deploy so merged code that didn't go live is visible
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: PAN-1224's core ask — rebuild+restart the dashboard after a PR merges so UAT-on-main runs against merged code — is already shipped (PAN-444 added deploy step 0 to postMergeLifecycle; PAN-1723/1763/1764 hardened scripts/post-merge-deploy.sh). The one unaddressed slice is Q3: when the post-merge build FAILS (post-merge-deploy.sh exits non-zero before the restart marker and restart at :73/:82/:91), the old dashboard keeps running on STALE code and NO operator-visible signal fires (dashboard.lifecycle_failed is only emitted on a fresh boot that never happens, or by the in-process runner — neither is a build failure; the deacon-patrol fallback emits nothing). The operator silently UATs old code; the only trace is /tmp/overdeck-deploy.log.
narratives.Proposal: Add a cross-process 'dashboard is stale' file marker (~/.overdeck/dashboard-stale.json) that the failing deploy script writes (pre-dist-swap failures only) and the running dashboard surfaces two ways: (a) an error-level activity-feed entry via a new poll+dedupe watcher modelled on restart-announcer, and (b) a persistent top banner driven by GET /api/dashboard/stale. The marker is the single source of truth, owned by one server-lib module that the bash writer, the endpoint, the watcher, and `pan reload` all conform to. The marker clears on deploy-script success and on a successful `pan reload`.
narratives.NonGoals:
- Do NOT make the deacon-patrol fallback (deacon.ts:5007-5033) emit failure, add a stale-build watchdog, or add a pan doctor check (the rejected 'broader hardening' option).
- Do NOT write the marker on the health-check-timeout path (post-merge-deploy.sh:186): post-dist-swap the fresh code is on disk; that is the existing backend-offline banner's job.
- Do NOT change rebuild scope, restart strategy, sidecar boundaries, or any Q1/Q2/Q4/Q6 behavior — already correct.
- Do NOT add a browser button that triggers a rebuild; the remedy is `pan reload`.
- Do NOT touch remote/multi-user dashboards (issue Q5).
- Do NOT overload restart-status.json for the persistent stale state (wrong lifetime: single-slot, overwritten on next restart).
- Do NOT clear the marker on `pan restart` (no rebuild = does not fix staleness) or on plain server boot.

items.title: Add dashboard-stale-marker lib module (canonical schema + read/clear/write)

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/dashboard-stale-marker.ts (sibling of, and modelled on, src/lib/restart-status.ts). Export interface DashboardStaleMarker { issueId?: string; reason: string; stage: 'fetch'|'worktree'|'build'; error: string; logFile: string; timestamp: number }. Export STALE_MARKER_PATH = join(getOverdeckHome(), 'dashboard-stale.json') using getOverdeckHome() from ./paths.js. Implement readDashboardStaleMarker(): Promise<DashboardStaleMarker|null> with defensive field validation (ENOENT and any parse/validation failure -> null, copying the shape of readRestartStatusPromise at restart-status.ts:40-77); clearDashboardStaleMarker(): Promise<void> (unlink, ignore ENOENT); writeDashboardStaleMarker(marker): Promise<void> (atomic tmp-write + rename, mirror writeRestartStatusPromise). Add unit tests in src/lib/__tests__/dashboard-stale-marker.test.ts.

items.title: writeDashboardStaleMarker then readDashboardStaleMarker round-trips an identical DashboardStaleMarker object
readDashboardStaleMarker returns null when the file is absent (ENOENT) and when the file contains malformed JSON or a missing/typed-wrong required field
clearDashboardStaleMarker removes an existing marker file and resolves without throwing when the file is already absent
STALE_MARKER_PATH resolves to <overdeck-home>/dashboard-stale.json via getOverdeckHome()
post-merge-deploy.sh writes the stale marker on pre-swap failure, clears on success

narrative.Action: In scripts/post-merge-deploy.sh define STALE_MARKER="$HOME/.overdeck/dashboard-stale.json" next to RESTART_MARKER (:22). Add a write_stale_marker <stage> <msg> helper that writes JSON matching the DashboardStaleMarker schema (issueId from $ISSUE_ID, reason from $REASON, stage, error truncated to ~200 chars, logFile=$LOG_FILE, timestamp via $(date +%s000) as the restart marker does at :119). Call write_stale_marker before the three pre-dist-swap failure exits: git fetch fail (:73, stage=fetch), git worktree add fail (:82, stage=worktree), npm run build fail (:91, stage=build). Do NOT call it on the health-check-timeout exit (:186). On the success path, before exit 0 (:181), run rm -f "$STALE_MARKER".

items.title: A forced `npm run build` failure creates ~/.overdeck/dashboard-stale.json containing stage=build, the issueId, logFile=/tmp/overdeck-deploy.log, and a numeric timestamp
The git-fetch and git-worktree failure exits write the marker with stage=fetch and stage=worktree respectively
A successful deploy run (exit 0) deletes ~/.overdeck/dashboard-stale.json
The health-check-timeout path exits 1 without creating the stale marker file
Add GET /api/dashboard/stale read endpoint

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/misc.ts add a GET /api/dashboard/stale route (HttpRouter.add + jsonResponse, following the existing route + Layer-merge pattern in this module). Handler returns jsonResponse(marker ? { stale: true, ...marker } : { stale: false }) where marker = await readDashboardStaleMarker() from src/lib/dashboard-stale-marker.js. Register the route in the module's exported Layer alongside the existing routes.

items.title: GET /api/dashboard/stale returns { stale: true, issueId, stage, error, logFile, timestamp } when the marker file is present
GET /api/dashboard/stale returns { stale: false } when no marker file exists
The endpoint responds 200 and does not throw when the marker file is malformed (degrades to { stale: false })
Stale-deploy watcher emits one deduped error activity entry per new marker

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/stale-deploy-watcher.ts modelled on restart-announcer.ts. Export announceNewStaleDeploy(deps?) (single pass; injectable readMarker/emit/getLastAnnounced/setLastAnnounced) and startStaleDeployWatcher()/stopStaleDeployWatcher() (poll loop with unref()ed timers and a bootstrap-fast then steady cadence, copying the announcer's structure). Read via readDashboardStaleMarker(); dedupe by marker.timestamp persisted in control-settings key 'stale_deploy_watcher.last_announced_ts' (getSetting/setSetting from ../../../lib/overdeck/control-settings.js). On a new marker emit emitActivityEntrySync({ source: 'deploy-script', level: 'error', message: a standalone sentence naming the issue and the `pan reload` remedy, details: error plus log path, issueId }). Wire startStaleDeployWatcher() into src/dashboard/server/main.ts next to startRestartAnnouncer() (:517) and stopStaleDeployWatcher() next to stopRestartAnnouncer() (:504). Add a unit test driving announceNewStaleDeploy with injected deps; use vi.useFakeTimers() for any loop-cadence assertions.

items.title: Given a marker whose timestamp has not been announced, announceNewStaleDeploy emits exactly one activity entry with level 'error' and source 'deploy-script' and returns true
Given a marker whose timestamp equals the persisted last-announced value, announceNewStaleDeploy emits nothing and returns false
Given no marker present, announceNewStaleDeploy emits nothing and returns false
The emitted activity message is a complete standalone sentence that contains the merged issue id and the literal remedy text `pan reload`
Frontend hook + persistent StaleDashboardBanner

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/hooks/useDashboardStaleStatus.ts mirroring hooks/useCodexAuthStatus.ts: a typed fetch of GET /api/dashboard/stale wrapped in useQuery({ queryKey: ['dashboard-stale'], refetchInterval: 10_000, staleTime: 0 }). Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/StaleDashboardBanner.tsx mirroring components/CodexAuthBanner.tsx: returns null unless data?.stale; otherwise a persistent top banner reusing the destructive utility classes already used by the backend-offline banner (App.tsx:1410, bg-destructive/15 border-b-2 border-destructive/50 ...) with a standalone message naming the issue and the `pan reload` remedy (no action button). Render <StaleDashboardBanner /> in the banner stack in src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx (near :1383-1438); it must be independent of dashboardLifecycle.active so it does not collide with the existing 'Dashboard is restarting' banner. Add a component test in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/__tests__/StaleDashboardBanner.test.tsx.

items.title: When the stale endpoint reports { stale: true, issueId }, StaleDashboardBanner renders a banner containing the issue id and the text 'pan reload'
When the stale endpoint reports { stale: false }, StaleDashboardBanner renders nothing
The banner renders with the destructive banner styling and remains independent of the existing dashboardLifecycle restart banner (neither banner suppresses the other)
pan reload clears the stale marker on success

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/reload.ts, inside recordReloadStatus(startedAt, success, error) (:63-75), when success === true also `await clearDashboardStaleMarker()` (imported from ../../lib/dashboard-stale-marker.js). This single chokepoint covers both success branches — the pan-dev SIGUSR2 path (:102) and the detached-production path (:161). Do not clear on failure. Extend the existing reload test (src/cli/commands/__tests__/reload.test.ts) to assert the clear-on-success behavior.

items.title: A successful `pan reload` (success=true) deletes an existing ~/.overdeck/dashboard-stale.json via clearDashboardStaleMarker
A failed `pan reload` (success=false) preserves the stale marker (the marker file is not deleted)
